description: Airlines must assess their operation and ensure conformity with IOSA Standards and Recommended Practices (ISARPs).
Airline requests an audit by a third-party Audit Organization (AO).
The Audit Organization conducts a 5-day on-site audit to verify implementation of ISARPs.
Airline must address and close all non-conformities found during the audit.
Audit report is submitted to IATA for Quality Assurance (QA) review.
IATA verifies that all audit procedures were correctly followed.
The airline is added to the IOSA registry and receives its certificate.
Registration is valid for two years and the renewal audit must be completed before expiration.
